evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: An eye for data quality and structure — you care about getting things right, not just an answer
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Data quality and trustworthiness are named as core values alongside 'fast-paced environment' and rapid commercial storytelling, giving roughly equal weight to rigour and velocity.
evidence.domain_risk: a consumer credit broker powerhouse... helping them make informed decisions, access the right products, and improve their financial wellbeing
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Intelligent Lending is a consumer credit broker in regulated UK financial services, an override signal for high domain risk even though the JD itself is not compliance-heavy.
evidence.collaboration_width: data scientists; analytics and AI engineers; business teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: the data foundations the team relies on
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: An existing Central Intelligence Unit with data scientists, analytics/AI engineers, and named tooling already in place, alongside a mandate to extend into new products, indicates a mid-stage, growing function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Creating and maintaining BI chatbots that leverage curated datasets to empower non-technical business stakeholders to self-serve
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific, unusual technical detail (BI chatbots, named tools, the CIU/Ocean Squad structure) gives a concrete Tuesday-morning picture, consistent with hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: translating these findings into clear and actionable commercial recommendations for stakeholders
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role explicitly serves commercial/marketing outcomes (product performance, marketing efforts, commercial recommendations) more than internal data or finance functions, a commercial orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Collaborating with business teams to understand their analytical needs and develop solutions that drive business value
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The entry-level role responds to business team needs and delivers scoped analyses/dashboards rather than setting strategy, an execution framing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI/ML is mentioned as a company capability (AI engineers, predictive analytics initiatives) but no AI tool usage or AI-facing infrastructure building is expected of this candidate specifically.
evidence.testing_framing: An eye for data quality and structure — you care about getting things right, not just an answer
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality is named as a desired trait/mindset rather than a formal testing framework the candidate owns, closer to tool_listed.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and trustworthiness of data are named concerns but framed around dashboard/insight quality rather than explicit regulatory or compliance risk, placing it at moderate despite the fintech sector.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Evaluating the potential impact of greenfield projects, such as the integration of new products, verticals, or customer journeys
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both maintains existing data foundations and explicitly takes on greenfield projects for new products/verticals, a genuine mix.
evidence.loss_aversion: clean, reliable, well-modelled datasets — reliability framing centers on trustworthy insights rather than explicit compliance risk.
evidence.work_arrangement: This is a hybrid role that sits between analytics engineering and data analysis.
